name: Reclaim.ai API
what_it_does: Reclaim.ai API — AI-powered calendar scheduling that automatically finds optimal times for tasks, habits, and meetings by analyzing calendar availability, enabling agents to intelligently schedule work and protect focus time.
use_cases: Agents scheduling recurring tasks and habits automatically in user calendars based on priority and availability
Smart meeting scheduling — agents using Reclaim to find optimal meeting slots that protect deep work blocks
Task time blocking — agents converting project tasks from Asana/Linear into Reclaim calendar blocks with smart scheduling
Buffer time management — agents configuring Reclaim to automatically add travel and prep time around meetings
Productivity analytics — agents querying Reclaim for time allocation data to report on focus time vs meeting ratios
not_for: Simple calendar booking pages — use Calendly for straightforward external meeting scheduling links
Enterprise meeting room booking — use Microsoft Bookings or Robin for enterprise room/resource scheduling
Non-Google Calendar users — Reclaim is Google Calendar-native; Outlook users should use Microsoft Scheduler
best_when: You need AI-powered intelligent time blocking for individuals and teams who want to automatically protect focus time while keeping calendars balanced.
avoid_when: You need external scheduling links for prospects or clients — use Calendly or Chili Piper for external-facing booking pages.

auth.notes: API token from Reclaim account settings. Bearer token in Authorization header. Account-wide access. Token tied to specific user's calendar and tasks.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 3 habits, basic task scheduling, 1 scheduling link; Lite: more habits and integrations
pricing.notes: API access available on all plans. Free plan has feature restrictions but API is accessible. Team features require Business plan.

performance.rate_limits: No published rate limits; practical limits apply; scheduling operations are async

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Swagger/OpenAPI spec available but documentation is sparse. Error responses include messages. API is evolving — some endpoints may change. Scheduling operations are asynchronous.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Task creation is not idempotent — duplicates can be created. Updates by task ID are idempotent. Scheduling decisions are made asynchronously by Reclaim's AI.
known_agent_gotchas: Scheduling is AI-driven and asynchronous — agents cannot predict exact calendar block times after task creation
Reclaim requires Google Calendar connection — only works for Google Calendar users; no Outlook support
API is less mature than calendar-focused alternatives — expect less comprehensive error handling and documentation
Task priorities and durations influence AI scheduling — agents must understand Reclaim's priority system for accurate scheduling
Rate limits undocumented — implement conservative pacing; AI scheduling operations are heavier than simple CRUD

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II. GDPR compliant. Bearer token with account-wide access and no scope granularity. US data processing. Calendar data is sensitive — handle API tokens carefully.
